what_happened: Workflow had workflow-level permissions (contents: write, pull-requests: write) but all write operations used a GitHub App token or GH_PAT secret, not GITHUB_TOKEN
why: GitHub Actions GITHUB_TOKEN permissions are separate from secrets. The gh CLI uses GH_TOKEN/GITHUB_TOKEN env var when set, but in this workflow every gh command explicitly sets GH_TOKEN to the App token or PAT secret. Only actions/checkout uses GITHUB_TOKEN implicitly.
fix_applied: Removed workflow-level permissions block, added job-level permissions block with only contents: read, and added inline comment explaining why write permissions are not needed
pattern_to_remember: In GitHub Actions workflows: audit whether GITHUB_TOKEN is actually used for write operations before granting write permissions. If all gh CLI calls set GH_TOKEN explicitly to an App token or PAT, the GITHUB_TOKEN only needs contents: read for checkout. Always prefer job-level permissions over workflow-level to apply least privilege.
